publishedAbstract: This document outlines the process of setting up dynamic NFS storage provisioning in OpenShift, a critical component for continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD) tools like Tekton and Jenkins that require persistent storage. Dynamic storage provisioning automates the creation and management of storage volumes, essential for efficient resource management in containerized environments. The guide covers the setup of an NFS server, the configuration of the NFS Subdirectory External Provisioner within OpenShift, and the deployment of a StorageClass for dynamic provisioning of PersistentVolumeClaims (PVCs). This setup enables seamless integration of NFS storage with OpenShift, facilitating the deployment of applications that require persistent data storage. Additionally, the document provides instructions for integrating this storage solution with Tekton and Jenkins, allowing these CI/CD tools to use dynamically provisioned storage, thus optimizing and automating workflows in OpenShift environments.
publishedTitle: Configuring Dynamic NFS Storage Provisioning in OpenShift for CI/CD Workflows
